mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL > 244) combined with a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS = 0.65%) creates a negative real rate environment. The Fed will be forced to raise rates aggressively, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as market participants price in future tightening and inflation persistence.
evidence_refs: fact:Real rates deeply negative at decision point
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation above target
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline over 20-day forward window despite trigger activation in > 40% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment (PAYEMS = 145,554k) and low unemployment (UNRATE = 4.8%) signal a tight labor market with persistent wage pressure. This sustains inflation expectations and forces the Fed to maintain or increase rates, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher as the market reprices terminal rate expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market remains resilient despite Fed tightening
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields fall over 20-day window in > 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between labor tightness and yield direction becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS = 472,142M) remain elevated, indicating persistent consumer demand despite Fed tightening. This sustains growth expectations and inflation, requiring higher terminal rates. The 10-year Treasury yield rises as the market prices in a higher neutral rate and longer duration of restrictive policy.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending resilience amid rate hikes
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day Treasury yield changes falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or yields decline in > 55% of months with above-median RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO = 104.55) shows supply-side resilience. Combined with persistent inflation, this signals stagflation risk where the Fed cannot ease without reigniting price pressures. The 10-year Treasury yield rises as the market prices in a higher terminal rate and extended tightening cycle to combat supply-constrained inflation.
evidence_refs: fact:Supply-side inflation persists despite demand moderation
regime_hint: stagflation regime with constrained supply and elevated inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline over 60-day forward window in > 45% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between INDPRO and CPIAUCSL shows Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST = 1,226k) decline sharply from historical averages as mortgage rates rise. This signals demand destruction in the housing sector, reducing growth expectations and inflation pressures. The 10-year Treasury yield falls as the market reprices terminal rate expectations downward and prices in a potential growth slowdown.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market cooling as mortgage rates rise
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with demand destruction
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise over 20-day window in > 50% of instances where HOUST < 1300, or Rank IC between HOUST and yield direction remains below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE = 4.8%) begins to rise above 5.0% threshold, signaling labor market deterioration. This reduces inflation expectations and growth forecasts, prompting market expectations of Fed rate cuts. The 10-year Treasury yield falls as terminal rate expectations decline and risk-free rates compress.
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market softening signals demand weakness
regime_hint: post-tightening cycle with emerging labor market slack
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise over 5-day window in > 55% of instances where UNRATE > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1 = 16,804.8B) contracts or stalls while inflation remains elevated (CPIAUCSL > 243). This stagflation signal forces the Fed to choose between fighting inflation or supporting growth. The 10-year Treasury yield falls as the market prices in eventual policy reversal and recession risk, despite near-term tightening.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise over 60-day window in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS = 0.65%) remains at emergency levels while inflation (CPIAUCSL = 244.158) is at 40-year highs. The policy rate is deeply negative in real terms. Market participants expect rapid Fed tightening to normalize rates. The 10-year Treasury yield rises sharply as the market front-runs the Fed's tightening cycle and reprices the entire yield curve upward.
evidence_refs: fact:Deeply negative real rates at decision point
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation shock
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline over 5-day window in > 60% of observations, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and yield direction changes falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
